commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2f9fa1a295d3e926e43b44348063e85254c247cd)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a bounded 16 MiB read/parse path for GitHub Copilot OAuth/API JSON responses, adds an oversized-response regression test, and adds a root PR draft Markdown file.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +22, Docs +46. Total +81 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review. Current main and v2026.6.10 still use unbounded response.text()/response.json() in the core Copilot helper, and the PR body includes terminal proof for an over-limit response stream.
commands.review_followup: - A repair worker can delete the committed root PR draft file; the runtime hardening still needs normal maintainer review and exact-head checks.

target.title: fix(copilot-oauth): bound OAuth and API endpoint response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`fetchJson` in `src/llm/utils/oauth/github-copilot.ts` reads the GitHub Copilot
OAuth and API endpoint response bodies with unbounded `await response.text()`
and `await response.json()`. A compromised or hijacked endpoint can stream an
arbitrarily large body and force the runtime to buffer the entire payload — an
OOM/DoS vector.

This is the same vulnerability class already hardened in the Anthropic OAuth
sibling (`src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts`, PR #96644).

## Changes

- `src/llm/utils/oauth/github-copilot.ts` — import `readResponseWithLimit`
  from `@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit`; replace both
  `await response.text()` and `await response.json()` with
  `readResponseWithLimit` at 16 MiB cap + `new TextDecoder().decode(buffer)` +
  `JSON.parse`. Uses `TextDecoder` to preserve UTF-8 BOM stripping semantics
  that `response.text()` provides.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

```text
$ node --import tsx /tmp/verify-copilot-oauth-overflow.mjs
PASS: readResponseWithLimit threw: GitHub Copilot token refresh request response too large: 16777217 bytes
PASS: pullCount = 1 (<= 2 means stream cancelled early)
PASS: stream cancelled = true
```

A ReadableStream enqueues a single 16777217 byte chunk (just over the 16 MiB
cap) into a Response body. `readResponseWithLimit` detects the overflow, throws
via `onOverflow`, and cancels the underlying stream — `pullCount = 1` proves no
further chunks were read, and `cancelled = true` proves the stream was cleaned
up.

### Tests

- **New Vitest case `rejects an oversized Copilot token refresh response`**
  creates a ReadableStream whose first chunk exceeds the 16 MiB cap and proves
  the bounded read cancels the body (`cancel` called once, `pull` count ≤ 2)
  and throws an error containing `"too large"`.
- **Existing 10 Vitest cases unchanged** — device flow, token refresh, model
  listing, timeout, and unsafe lifetime rejection all pass.
- `pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/github-copilot.test.ts` → **11 passed (11)**
target.author: solodmd
